purpose: Roadmap for agent-facing motion design skills, SVG/HTML/CSS/Remotion animation planning, smoothness review, and publish-prep integration.

artifacts.purpose: Reusable skill that routes SVG, HTML/CSS, and Remotion animation work through motion briefs, token reuse, and validation.
Record animation surface, duration, visual goal, beat frames, moving elements, safe zones, blocked effects, and review frames.
Record which skill, command, references, source docs, and constraints guided generated animation code.
Review generated animation code for frame timing, token use, transform/opacity preference, layout stability, and performance risks.
Tie generated animation to timing, easing, spring, and custom motion tokens.
Plan Remotion sequences, transitions, springs, interpolations, caption safe zones, and performance budget.
Plan SVG viewBox, animated elements, path draws, masks, transforms, text strategy, and fallback.
Plan HTML/CSS selectors, CSS variables, keyframes, transform-only checks, and blocked properties.
Index screenshot or still-frame samples for representative motion frames.
Check jitter, layout shift, unexpected jumps, transition coherence, and motion density.
Verify moving elements do not collide with captions or platform safe zones.
Identify GPU-heavy effects, layout animation, external fetches, expensive JS, uncached assets, and recommended precomputes.
Aggregate motion plan, code review, frame samples, smoothness, safe-zone, and performance reports into pass/revise/fail.

gates: Animation code must be frame-driven or explicitly keyframed.
Motion plans must protect caption and platform safe zones.
Agents should use repo timing, easing, and spring tokens before inventing new values.
Motion-heavy output requires representative frame review, not only a final MP4 existence check.
Publish prep should fail janky, unreadable, unsafe, or render-expensive motion.
